# testdata — regression permanence

## fixture.db (synthetic)

A fully synthetic ~400-fact oracle DB generated by
`scripts/make_synthetic_fixture.py` — fake projects, fake boxes, TEST-NET IPs,
supersession chains, entities, ask traces, and deterministic hash-embedding
vectors. No real session content.

CI runs `oracle selfeval --fixture testdata/fixture.db` against it (with the
same script serving query embeddings via `--serve`) and fails if the era or
continuity hit rates drop below `thresholds.json`.

Regenerate:

    go build -o oracle-bin .
    python3 scripts/make_synthetic_fixture.py --oracle ./oracle-bin --out testdata/fixture.db
    python3 scripts/make_synthetic_fixture.py --serve 8901 &
    ORACLE_EMBED_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8901/embeddings \
      ./oracle-bin selfeval --fixture testdata/fixture.db --sample 100 --json

then set `thresholds.json` a few points below the measured rates.

## canary_chunks/ + canary_expected.json

Three fixed hand-written claude-code-format transcripts. `oracle canary` runs
real LLM extraction on each and requires every expected fact (a keyword set in
`canary_expected.json`) to appear in at least one extracted statement — all
keywords, case-insensitive. Keywords are deliberately loose (3-4 per fact) to
survive phrasing variation; regenerate candidates with `oracle canary --gen`.
